summary:
The most common site for melanotic neuroectodermal tumour of infancy (MNTI) is the maxilla, based on its association with head and neck surgical interventions frequently described in the context of such tumors. Despite lacking direct textual confirmation, the maxilla is inferred as the primary location due to procedural ties within the head/neck region and established medical knowledge aligning with these findings.  

(Note: This summary adheres solely to the critical clinical rationale presented by Agent2.)
